operating_state: Second quarter 2023 earnings of $7.9 billion decreased from $17.9 billion in the prior year, driven by 33% lower crude oil realizations and 51% lower natural gas realizations. Upstream production of 3.6 million oil-equivalent barrels per day declined 124 thousand barrels daily from 2022, though net production increased excluding entitlements and divestments. Energy Products earnings fell on lower industry refining margins, partially offset by the Beaumont refinery expansion and increased marketing contributions.
cost_structure: Capital and exploration expenditures increased 34% to $6.2 billion in the second quarter, with upstream spending rising significantly from 2022 levels. Chemical Products margins weakened on bottom-of-cycle conditions in Asia Pacific, while Specialty Products margins strengthened on lower feed costs. Corporate and financing expenses increased reflecting unfavorable tax items and foreign exchange impacts partially offset by lower financing costs.
material_events: ExxonMobil announced a definitive agreement to acquire Denbury Inc. on July 13, 2023, to accelerate Low Carbon Solutions opportunities. The company distributed dividends and repurchased common stock totaling over $16 billion during the first six months. European Member States imposed additional taxes on energy sector companies, resulting in after-tax charges.
macro_exposures: Crude oil prices remained relatively flat in the second quarter after OPEC+ producers reduced output to offset concerns over weakening global economic conditions. Natural gas storage levels increased above historical averages in the United States and Europe on higher supply and lower demand. Chemical margins remained well below the 10-year range due to continued bottom-of-cycle conditions in Asia Pacific.
forward_outlook: The Corporation plans to invest $23 billion to $25 billion in 2023, with actual spending varying based on project progress and property acquisitions. Management maintains capital allocation priorities of investing in competitively advantaged high-return projects, maintaining a strong balance sheet, and sharing success through consistent share repurchases and growing dividends.
uncertainty_profile: The company faces ongoing litigation and contingencies as disclosed in financial statement notes. Long-term contractual obligations total approximately $6.9 billion through July 2023. The Denbury acquisition remains subject to regulatory approvals and other closing conditions, with relevant SEC filings pending.
